goal: Verify that the newly created multi-agent prompts, schema, and policies implement the documented prompt stack, structured output contract, memory write boundaries, injection safeguards, and fallback handling.
scope: Inspect the provided prompt, schema, and policy files to determine whether they collectively satisfy the subagent contract requirements without modifying any workspace files.
summary: The shared base contract defines the five-layer prompt stack, role skeleton, JSON-only output rules, recovery/fallback behavior, and context safety expectations; the schema enforces the required fields, evidence typing, fallback enum, and confidence bounds; the memory policy delegates long-term writes to the main agent with entropy-handling strategies; and the injection policy mandates treating external content as data, adhering to tool/role limits, and reporting out-of-scope situations via risks or next steps.

evidence.value: /Users/zyh/Desktop/ceshi123/ollamashiyong/codex-global-multi-agent/prompts/shared/base_contract.md documents the prompt stack, role format requirements, JSON-only output enforcement, recovery rules, and context safety limitations.

/Users/zyh/Desktop/ceshi123/ollamashiyong/codex-global-multi-agent/schemas/subagent_contract.schema.json enforces the mandatory goal/scope/allowed_tools/summary/evidence/state_delta/risks/fallback/next_steps/confidence fields with strict typing and fallback enum.

/Users/zyh/Desktop/ceshi123/ollamashiyong/codex-global-multi-agent/policies/memory_write_policy.md forbids child agents from writing long-term memory or global skill configs, leaving state_delta review and memory resolution to the main agent.

/Users/zyh/Desktop/ceshi123/ollamashiyong/codex-global-multi-agent/policies/injection_boundary.md describes treating external content as data, staying within tool/context boundaries, degrading confidence when evidence is missing, and flagging overreach via risks/next_steps.

facts: Prompt stack, role structure, output/recovery/fallback, and context safety expectations are explicitly documented in the base contract.
The JSON schema enforces every required subagent output field, evidence typing, and constrained fallback options.
Memory writes are restricted to candidate/state_delta submissions, with the main agent owning deduplication and entropy resolution.
Injection boundaries require data treatment of external content, tool whitelists, and explicit handling of out-of-scope or missing-evidence situations.
preferences: Maintain structured JSON output with clear indication of actual evidence versus inference, aligning with the schema and base contract.
When missing evidence, lower confidence and offer fallback suggestions rather than forcing assumptions.
decisions: Report compliance findings strictly via the mandated JSON contract for downstream consumers.
risks: Policies mitigate unauthorized memory writes and instruction injection; no additional immediate risks were discovered.
